content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1109 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660

EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

January 16, 1976

Senator John Nejedly
7th District
State Capitol
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Senator Nejedly:

Thank you for your letters of January 7, 1976, and
January 12, 1976, concerning information to be included
on tickets to political fundraising dinners. My under-
standing is that you are seeking advice only as to the
statements attached to your letter of January 12. I can-
not comment on the statements concerning tax treatment of
contributions but the information about campaign reporting
requirements is, with one exception, correct. The state-
ment about reports by contributors is not complete since
a "committee" which purchased a ticket to a dinner would
be required to report the purchase as a political expendi-
ture. Individuals or corporations, on the other hand, which
do not receive contributions or make expenditures on behalf
of a candidate, would not incur any reporting obligations
until they had made contributions directly to candidates
or committees of $5,000 or more in a calendar year.

I hope the foregoing is helpful. If you have any
further questions, please contact Ted Prim, an attorney
with the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DHL:pvp

FOR CONSIDERATION
AT OPINION REQUEST
MEETING

JOHN A. NEJEDLY
SEVENTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT
CONTRA COSTA COUNTY

SENATE SELECT COMMITTEE ON
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
SENATE SELECT COMMITTEE ON
TAXATION OF TIMBER AND
TIMBERLAND
JOINT COMMITTEE ON FAIRS
ALLOCATION AND
CLASSIFICATION
JOINT COMMITTEE ON
REVISION OF THE
PENAL CODE
WILDLIFE CONSERVATION
BOARD

CALIFORNIA LEGISLATURE

Senate

January 7, 1976


Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J Street, Suite 522
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

I understand from your staff that information
that I had placed on tickets to be sold for
a testimonial dinner is appropriate.

I would appreciate your advising me if you
find anything inappropriate with any of
these statements.

Very truly yours,

JOHN A. NEJEDLY
Senator, 7th District

Enclosure
JAN:jn

Federal - Ordinal of contribution as tax credit up
to a maximum of $25, single, or $50 on joint return.
tax deduction as contribution to political campaign
up to $100 single or $200 on joint return.

State - Tax deduction as contribution to political
campaign up to $100 single or $200 on joint return.

CAMPAIGN REPORT REQUIREMENT

Amounts paid for these tickets and any other contributions to the Senator's campaign will be reported by
the Campaign Committee.

No report need be filed by any contributor because
of contributions made to the Senator unless your
total contribution (all campaigns) exceed $5000 during
the year 1976.

This is a State campaign and corporate contributions
are permissible.

Sanford H. Skaggs
Treasurer

Limited version

contributions but the information about campaign reporting
requirements is, with one exception, correct. The state-
ment about reports by contributors is not complete since

ture. Individuals or corporations, on the other hand, which
do not receive contributions or make expenditures on behalf
of a candidate, would not incur any reporting obligations
until they had made contributions directly to candidates
or committees of $5,000 or more in a calendar year.

I hope the foregoing is helpful. If you have any
further questions, please contact Ted Prim, an attorney
with the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

embedding.qa_text: January 16, 1976 Senator John Nejedly 7th District State Capitol Sacramento CA 95814 Dear Senator Nejedly: Thank you for your letters of January 7, 1976, and January 12, 1976, concerning information to be included on tickets to political fundraising dinners. My understanding is that you are seeking advice only as to the statements attached to your letter of January 12. I cannot comment on the statements concerning tax treatment of contributions but the information about campaign reporting requirements is, with one exception, correct. The statement about reports by contributors is not complete since a "committee" which purchased a ticket to a dinner would be required to report the purchase as a political expenditure. Individuals or corporations, on the other hand, which do not receive contributions or make expenditures on behalf of a candidate, would not incur any reporting obligations until they had made contributions directly to candidates or committees of $5,000 or more in a calendar year. I hope the foregoing is helpful. If you have any further questions, please contact Ted Prim, an attorney with the Commission staff. Sincerely, Daniel H. Lowenstein Chairman This is a letter from Senator John A. Nejedly to Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman of the Fair Political Practices Commission. The letter is dated January 7, 1976, and is written in response to information that Senator Nejedly had placed on tickets for a testimonial dinner. The content of the letter states: "I understand from your staff that information that I had placed on tickets to be sold for a testimonial dinner is appropriate. I would appreciate your advising me if you find anything inappropriate with any of these statements." The senator expresses his appreciation for Mr. Lowenstein's advice and concludes with "Very truly yours," followed by his signature and title as Senator, 7th District. Enclosure: JAN:jn TAX TREATMENT Federal - One-half of contribution as tax credit up to a maximum of $25, single, or $50 on joint return. Tax deduction as contribution to political campaign up to $100 single or $200 on joint return. State - Tax deduction as contribution to political campaign up to $100 single or $200 on joint return. CAMPAIGN REPORT REQUIREMENT Amounts paid for these tickets and any other contributions to the Senator's campaign will be reported by the Campaign Committee. No report need be filed by any contributor because of contributions made to the Senator unless your total contribution (all campaigns) exceed $5000 during the year 1976. This is a State campaign and corporate contributions are permissible. Sanford M.
